mission.title: node-exporter: Filesystems mounted while node exporter is running are not detected
mission.description: Filesystems mounted while node exporter is running are not detected. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current node-exporter deployment
steps.description:
Verify your node-exporter version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
helm list -n node-exporter 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working node-exporter installation.

steps.title: Review node-exporter configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant node-exporter configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get configmap -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=node-exporter
```
Metrics did not show up for a new filesystem when I mounted it (waited for over an hour before investigating further). After restarting node-exporter, it appears fine.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Filesystems mounted while node exporter is running are not…
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n node-exporter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=node-exporter
kubectl get events -n node-exporter --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Filesystems mounted while node exporter is running are not…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/prometheus/node_exporter/pull/660

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with node-exporter installed or the issue environment reproducible.
